item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The ambiguity makes this one for me. The fact that the 'monster' could be:
1. A result of sensory deprivation and thus entirely nonexistent.
2. An anomalous property concurrent with the loss of sight/sound or
3. A straight up scary beastie with the power to create areas of silence or darkness
is just fantastic. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creepy. I like it. Still disapointed the words Mole Rats were there but there was no reference to Zeta-9.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Four!

item.title: Image Removal
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1303's image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact Fantem or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

Image found here:
http://bonnjo2.deviantart.com/art/Cavern-82777852

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like everything up to the part where it says no post mentioning the caverns was found on the site. I just don't care for the whole "This was where we found it, but it isn't here any more" twist, I guess. I personally like the part where, despite safety gear, the girl died of massive trauma, like maybe something actually beat her skull in against the stalagmite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, any of those circumstances are good. I'm going with the final for the actual explanation. Well, unless it's a creature that exists only as a hallucination (that is, a person hallucinating in this cave will see/hear it, and thus everyone sees/hears the same creature).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it was a carnivorous creature, maybe. But if it's, say, a phobophage, getting sustenance from the fear and panic it creates, having the effects trigger separately would be a better hunting ground. Or maybe there's no creature at all, and it really is just all in the subjects' minds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Too bad they don't overlap. I was imagining this being some thing's haphazard hunting mechanism, and when it rolls doubles, it gets free reign to snack on whatever's in that cave.

Also herp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With the way the guy is so calmly answering the questions (plus all the "Sir"s and what not) I wouldn't mind the interview concluding with some sort of "The subject was detained to allow for a brief background check, and was subsequently recruited and placed into a newly forming MTF with emphasis on subterranean exploration" or something. That would kinda be cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In order…

1) That's deliberate - SCP-1303 is the cave system as a whole, but the individual caves within it are unremarkable. I suppose they could be numbered SCP-1303-1 through -11, and the two phenomena altered to be SCP-1303-A and 1303-B, but leaving the phenomena as -1 and -2 and just referring to the caverns as 'Chamber 1 through 11 was less confusing.

2) I don't know. Which is creepier, to have a space that simply absorbs light/sound, or one that has light/sound just skip over it like it's not there?

3) If you have no light sources, what are you doing exploring a cave anyways? :) As for the sound, you might think that, but you'd be completely wrong. Read up on anechoic chambers, it's actually kind of fascinating - your body, unless born 100% deaf, depends on minor echoes for things like your sense of balance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ted's not an SCP, it's a semi-famous creepypasta, originally posted on the actual NSS forums. Not sure where the best place to find it now is, but Google would probably help.

The two phenomena are supposed to never be overlapping - I had that in an earlier draft, but it looks like I forgot it. Will fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely fail to see the usefulness of designating the effects with sub SCP numbers. they are in absolutely no way separate from the caves, but distinct anomalous properties of/phenomenas in it. There is no season whatsoever for this numbering. (If the individual caverns where numbered in some fashion, now THAT is useful since they are affected independently of each others by the two phenomena and need to be readily identified!)

Does 1303 affect non-acoustic transmissions travelling through it as opposed to being emitted or recorded within it (i.e. starting outside the area on one end and attempted to be recorded outside it on the other end)? Also would it affect things like attempt to transmit via SMS/IM-like communications?.

IMO, it would be more interesting if the description made it as the second phenomenon being found out while investigating the first: absolute darkness in caves is not exactly new (If you have no lighting equipment, you cannot TELL when the darkness event occurs other than by the sound!). I thought the extra log wasn't that interesting an addition either, plus the "ever tried running when you can't hear your own footsteps, sir? It's freaky, and turns out it's really d—— hard." bit? Makes no sense whatsoever. The only problem, unless the silence messes up with body placement sens, is that you have no understanding of where otehrs are wunless they are within your field of vision.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Scary, upvote for making me imagine myself in the cave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just okay, honestly.

What I want to know is, do the two phenomena ever intersect in a cave? The darkness one is ostensibly more dangerous, if you're trying to move quickly and can't coordinate, but if both senses went out, most people would be fucked.

Also, someone needs to point me at Ted the Caver. I have no idea which SCP that is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have new headcanon. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, it's a nice little Easter Egg. Perhaps some of them DID become members of the MTF later on… ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is Mole Rats the name of a MTF? I just thought it was the sort of nickname some amateur cavers would give themselves.

EDIT: Yup, Zeta-9. Color me purple for not doing my research beforehand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With the caver team name being "Mole Rats", I half expected the interviewee to be labeled as an Agent at the end of the interview on that team. Don't know if that reference was intentional or not, but even though I liked it I was disappointed by that not being the case. Regardless, well written, and a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That makes at least three of us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was alright with caves prior to reading this. Not so much now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't really think it's that interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting enough for me, you've won my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ^ That is exactly why I up-voted also.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Caves are scary. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of course. Where's the fun otherwise?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the ambiguity. Is it a monster, or a weird cave that makes you think there's a monster? Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
cause of death was determined to be cranial trauma caused when Ms. L██████ tripped and struck her head against a protruding stalagmite, despite her protective headgear.

Hmmm, so is it supposed to be ambiguous as to whether or not a monster is actually in there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Darnit. Thanks, fixing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad. You forgot to censor one Jeff, by the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's fine, I wouldn't expect unanimous approval in any case.

Honestly, I think Ted The Caver might end up being my biggest problem…people will see this and think 'Oh, it's like TtC, but not as good'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good, and you should feel really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like that it changes chambers at will, but history and research has shown the blocking of certain senses in a tight space does lead to hallucinations and paranoia anyway.
It's no Ted the Caver, but it's not something that should be hated either.
Like I mentioned, I dont dislike it nor like it, I remain squarely disinterested.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it! Also, it reminded me of Ted the Caver.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The perimeter is to keep people from entering it via another means. It doesn't do any good to block off the main entrance if someone, for whatever crazy reason, decides to go digging on the other side of the hill and accidentally tunnels in a new entryway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, 1303-1 isn't just anechoic, and 1302-1 isn't just pitch-dark. In both cases, the sense that wasn't suppressed gets subjected to hallucinations that may or may not be part of the anomalous effect. (The ambiguity of that is something I like quite a lot, personally.) Also which chamber is anechoic or pitch-dark at any given time, if any, keeps changing. That's definitely quite anomalous…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't care for it as the premise is rather boring (some place has no sound for a while, the other prevents sight). The containment is silly too, why leave it so easy to enter if you don't want people in there? Why not a barrier like some fence that blocks off the direct entrance?

Not a bad or good vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Darn, thanks for catching that. Fixing now.

Adding tags now.

item.title: Author Commentary
item.content:encoded:
Not really a lot to say here this time. The idea came initially from reading online about anechoic chambers and how they cause disorientation and hallucinations really fast, and that merged into brainstorming by comments from someone in chat about how we don't have very many underground SCPs.

Appreciation to Kamelov this time, giving the idea of a possibly hallucinatory creature that makes it more than just a weird cave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Text Describing The Picture
